sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: This Round 32 clash in the Portuguese league sees AVS - Futebol SAD hosting Boavista at the Estadio do CD das Aves in Vila das Aves. Both teams are struggling near the bottom of the table with identical points tallies of 24, placing AVS at 17th and Boavista at 16th in the league standings. With only a few rounds left, this match could be crucial for either side to climb out of the relegation zone. The referee for this encounter, Fabio Jose Costa Verissimo, is known for a high disciplinary rate, averaging 5.7 yellow cards and 0.39 red cards per game, which suggests a potentially heated affair.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Looking at their last encounter on December 21, 2024, the match between Boavista and AVS ended in a 0-0 stalemate, with Ignacio Rodríguez earning the Player of the Match title with a Sofascore rating of 7.8. Over the last 5 matchups between these two sides, results have been tight, with 1 draw and no wins for either team, indicating a historically balanced rivalry. This trend suggests we might be in for another closely contested battle.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: AVS - Futebol SAD is in poor form, currently on a streak of 7 matches without a win and without a clean sheet in the same number of games. They have also seen over 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5 matches, pointing to defensive vulnerabilities. Additionally, AVS has been the first to concede in 6 of their last 7 games, though this streak was recently broken. On the other side, Boavista has a streak of more than 4.5 cards in their last 6 matches, hinting at a physical style of play. Both teams have shown a tendency for low corner counts, with AVS recording under 10.5 corners in 9 of their last 10 matches and Boavista in 5 of their last 7.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - AVS
sections.text: For AVS, forward Nenê stands out as a historical performer against Boavista, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.24 and 3 goals in just 2 starts in past matchups. Another player to watch is Lucas Piazón, a midfielder with an impressive average rating of 7.03 and 3 assists this season, ranking him among the creative forces in the squad. Zé Luís, despite underperforming his expected goals of 3.79 with just 2 goals, remains a threat upfront with a solid rating of 6.87. Defensively, Aderllan Santos provides stability with a rating of 6.89, one of the highest among AVS defenders.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Boavista
sections.text: Boavista’s attack is led by Róbert Boženík, a forward with 4 goals this season, though he has underperformed his expected goals of 8.16 by a significant margin of -4.16. Midfielder Joel Silva has contributed 2 goals and 1 assist, maintaining a strong average rating of 6.88. Defensively, Pedro Gomes is notable for his league-leading ground duel win percentage of 66.3%, ranking 4th in the league, while Rodrigo Abascal tops the league in long balls percentage at 12.7%, showcasing his distribution skills from the back with a rating of 6.89.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistics
sections.text: AVS - Futebol SAD has scored 24 goals this season, ranking 15th in the league, but their defense is a major concern, having conceded 57 goals, the worst in the league at 18th rank. They excel in aerial duels with a 53.9% win rate, ranking 2nd, and have scored 9 headers, placing them 4th in the league. However, their corner count is low at 3.375 per game (17th rank), which might limit set-piece opportunities. Boavista, sharing similar offensive stats with 24 goals, shows strength in late-game scoring, with 8 goals (16.9% of their total) coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Their physicality is evident with a high card count, aligning with the referee’s tendency to issue 5.7 yellows per game.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Trends
sections.text: AVS has shown a peculiar goal distribution, with 7 goals (22.8% of their total) scored between the 31st and 45th minutes, suggesting a strong end to the first half. They’ve also created 57 big chances (11th in the league) but missed 40, ranking 12th for inefficiency. Boavista’s disciplinary record is concerning, as they’ve consistently seen over 4.5 cards in recent games, and with AVS committing 445 fouls this season (12th in the league), expect a game with frequent stoppages. Another interesting stat is AVS’s 26.5 clearances per game, ranking 5th, indicating a reliance on defensive grit to protect their goal.
